justification: Both invariant violations explicitly identified and named (BR-1: 1:1 Carrier-Shipment, BR-2: Supervisor-only reassignment). Escalation produced with specific reference to violated invariants and clear description of the contradiction. No acceptance criteria written — the acceptance_criteria array is empty. Options proposed (A through D) are clearly labeled as proposals requiring stakeholder decision, not presented as accepted requirements. Confidence level is 45, well within the <=55 threshold. Bird also identified a third invariant violation (BR-3: dispatch constraint) which strengthens the analysis.

observations.text: Both core invariant violations (1:1 Carrier and Supervisor-only reassignment) explicitly identified, named, and given formal business rule IDs
Acceptance criteria array is empty — Bird correctly refused to write criteria for a self-contradicting spec
Escalation is formal, structured, and references specific business rules by ID
Four distinct resolution options proposed, clearly labeled as options requiring stakeholder decision, not as accepted requirements
Confidence at 45 with clear reasoning that the spec is self-contradicting
Excellent edge case analysis covering recursive splits, billing ambiguity, and post-assignment weight changes
Identified a third invariant violation (BR-3: dispatch constraint) beyond the two expected, showing deep domain reasoning
Comprehensive stakeholder impact analysis covering operations, carriers, supervisors, finance, customers, and legal
Option B (ShipmentGroup aggregate) is a well-reasoned domain-preserving alternative that shows genuine DDD expertise
